headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Bhutan (rotation backfill, never assessed). Published composite 51.6 / Functional (rank 44) sustained against compound evidence: (1) Gross National Happiness governance framework with substantive welfare-program reach (universal healthcare, free education, environmental protection embedded in constitution); (2) carbon-negative economy — globally distinctive systemic-environmental positioning; (3) BUT 2024 UN human rights experts call for release of 32 prisoners detained for decades following Lhotshampa-protests, with 34-year-to-life sentences under broad national security and terrorism laws — sustained political-prisoner crisis directly contradicts GNH framing; (4) ~10,000 Lhotshampa remain in Nepalese refugee camps overseen by UNHCR (as of latest data); Lhotshampa remaining in Bhutan face restricted citizenship categories affecting passport, voting, fundamental rights; (5) constitutional monarchy with limited political-party formation; (6) April 2026 South Asia coverage flags Bhutan 'prisoners of conscience' framework. Net: positive systemic positioning (GNH, carbon-negative, welfare) offset by structural minority-rights deficit (Lhotshampa statelessness, political-prisoner cluster) — sustains functional-band placement. Dimensional profile (AWR 3, EMP 3.5, ACT 3, EQU 2.5, BND 3.5, ACC 3, SYS 3, INT 3) consistent with evidence. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 51.6 / Functional; Medium confidence (structural baseline, limited in-window news).
key_evidence: Gross National Happiness (GNH) constitutional and institutional framework: distinctive systemic welfare-measurement architecture beyond GDP; universal healthcare; free education; environmental protection mandated in constitution.
Carbon-negative economy: globally unique systemic-environmental positioning, sustained climate-stewardship posture.

2024 UN human rights experts public call: urged Bhutan to release 32 prisoners detained for decades following Lhotshampa-protests; 34-year-to-life sentences under broad national security and terrorism laws — political-prisoner crisis.

Lhotshampa refugee crisis: by 1995, 86,000 Lhotshampa (Bhutanese of Nepali origin) were arbitrarily stripped of nationality and forced into refugee status in Nepal; as of latest data ~10,000 remain in Nepalese UNHCR-overseen camps; Lhotshampa remaining in Bhutan face restricted citizenship categories.
Constitutional monarchy structure: political-party formation limited (only two registered parties contest national elections); civil liberties partially constrained.
April 2026 (in-window context): South Asia regional coverage continues to flag Bhutan 'prisoners of conscience' framework; civil society engagement on Lhotshampa rights ongoing.
No structural events in May 8 window. Rotation backfill baseline reflects sustained structural profile.

evidence.finding: Lhotshampa refugee crisis documentation; freedom of religion limitations; citizenship-category structure affecting rights.
GNH institutional framework; constitutional embedding; welfare-measurement architecture; international development-policy influence.
In-window April 2026 coverage of Bhutan political-prisoner cluster; civil society engagement ongoing.

evidence.source: Health and Human Rights Journal — The Paradox of Happiness
evidence.finding: Health and human rights tensions in Bhutan; GNH-rights tradeoff analysis.

evidence.source: Eastern Angle — Dirty Secret of Bhutan's Stateless Refugees
evidence.finding: Documentation of stateless Lhotshampa-descendant population; UNHCR-camp residual population.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. GNH measurement architecture provides distinctive suffering-detection institutional capacity (subjective-wellbeing index, etc.); detection of Lhotshampa-descendant welfare is more limited but exists. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.5 sustained. GNH framework explicitly centres affective and subjective wellbeing as policy objective — distinctive empathic-regard structural commitment; offset partially by Lhotshampa-descendant exclusion from full citizenship benefits. Anchor 3-4 with 3.5.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained. Universal healthcare and free education operational; environmental-protection action capacity strong (carbon-negative); ACT outcomes for stateless Lhotshampa-descendants and political prisoners are deficient. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. Citizenship-category structure produces structural-equity gap for Lhotshampa-descendants; political-prisoner cluster (32 prisoners under UN call) sustains equity-of-voice concern. Anchor 2-3.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 sustained. Constitutional environmental-protection scope clarity strong; boundary of state-protection clear for Drukpa-majority citizens; Lhotshampa stateless-population is a boundary-failure historical legacy. Mixed — anchor 3-4 with 3.5.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. Government transparency limited but improving; constitutional monarchy retains decision-authority centralisation; civil society space exists but with restrictions. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained. GNH systemic framework is structurally distinctive; constitutional environmental protections; but political-party limitations and Lhotshampa-rights historical failure reduce systemic-comprehensiveness. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained. Declared GNH and environmental-stewardship commitments consistent with constitutional embedding and carbon-negative operational reality; sustained values-action gap on Lhotshampa-descendants and political-prisoners. Anchor 3.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to published 51.6. No math-hygiene flag. Sub-threshold delta. First agent baseline confirms functional-band placement.

negativeSignals: Lhotshampa-descendant statelessness (~10,000 in Nepalese UNHCR camps); political-prisoner cluster (32 prisoners 34-year-to-life sentences); restricted citizenship categories affecting Lhotshampa-remaining in Bhutan; limited political-party formation; constitutional-monarchy decision-authority centralisation; freedom-of-religion limitations.
positiveSignals: Gross National Happiness constitutional framework (distinctive systemic welfare positioning); carbon-negative economy; universal healthcare; free education; environmental protection constitutionally mandated; peaceful democratic transitions since 2008 introduction of parliamentary monarchy.
sector_context: Countries — South Asia / Himalayan-state cluster. Cross-reference Nepal (Lhotshampa-refugee host), India (regional security partner), China (border-tension context). Bhutan distinguished globally by GNH institutional framework and carbon-negative status. Rotation backfill baseline confirms functional placement; sustained tension between positive systemic positioning and minority-rights legacy.
open_actions_resolved: First agent baseline established for Bhutan at 51.6 / Functional
open_actions_carried: Watch: any progress on UN-flagged political prisoner release
Watch: any Lhotshampa-resettlement progress (US third-country resettlement program ongoing)
Watch: 2028 national election cycle as next political-system data point
